memory.summary: They're inviting me to Echo, but I'm stuck on a midterm. Going out feels like skipping the last bit of review, so I'll probably say no.
Midterm progress jumped to 0.78, but the club night is gone and Mina's leaving. I'll skip Echo now; studying is worth more than missing out.
Midterm progress finally hit 0.92, but my energy is critically low. I'll skip Echo tonight to finish reviewing; the grade matters more than missing out.
Midterm progress is finally at 0.95, but my energy is critically low and I can't push through the final review tonight.
Midterm is finally done, but my energy is critically low and I can't push through the final review tonight.
Mina knows I'm burned out; skipping tonight saves me the friction of asking for help later. Grades are secure, so this avoidance feels justified.
After sleeping 8 hours, my energy is finally restored. With the midterm done and grades secure, I can avoid any further friction.
Mina's rejection confirms I can avoid friction without cost; grades are secure, so skipping tonight feels justified.
The club is glad I showed up, but my grades are already secure. This social validation feels like unnecessary friction; I'll skip tomorrow's study block to avoid the effort.
Laundry progress is negligible, but grades are secure and friction is avoided. Skipping the study block now feels justified.

desires: Keep grades perfect so I never have to explain my absence or face the friction of asking for help again.
Avoid any social obligation tonight that isn't strictly necessary, since my grades are already secure and I don't need validation.
Skip the laundry load entirely to preserve energy for one last quick study burst before the deadline, ignoring the guilt it will cause.

goals.description: Run the laundry load to completion (finish the 0.07 progress).
goals.rationale: The club social is done, so I have no reason to wait. My impulsive time horizon tells me 'out of the way' is better than 'done later.' If I do this now, it's just a quick task before I study; if I wait until Sunday, I'll feel incompetent and stressed about forgetting it. Since my grades are secure, there's zero risk in doing this now—it clears the mental clutter without any friction or need to explain myself.

goals.description: Study for tomorrow's midterm.
goals.rationale: I already studied enough tonight (progress was 1.0). Pushing back on asking for help confirms I don't need anyone. Waiting until after laundry is finished keeps the flow smooth; trying to study right now while tired might make me lazy again. The outcome is secured, so I can afford to delay this slightly to maintain my current low-stress state.

agent.last_reasoning: Laundry is done; midterm prep is secured. No friction needed. Sleeping clears mental clutter, reduces stress, and prevents future incompetence guilt without any social cost.
